title: Timeline Object
description: A collection of TimelineObjects in a parametric design.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteAllAfterMarker
methods.description: Deletes all objects in the timeline that are after the current position of the marker.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified item in the timeline using an index into the collection. The items are returned in the order they appear in the timeline.

methods.name: moveToBeginning
methods.description: Moves the marker to the beginning of the timeline.

methods.name: moveToEnd
methods.description: Moves the marker to the end of the timeline.

methods.name: movetoNextStep
methods.description: Moves the marker to the next step in the timeline.

methods.name: moveToPreviousStep
methods.description: Moves the marker to the previous step in the timeline.

methods.name: play
methods.description: Plays the timeline beginning at the current position of the marker.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of items in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: markerPosition
properties.description: Gets and sets the current position of the marker where 0 is at the beginning of the timeline and the value of Timeline.count is the end of the timeline.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: timelineGroups
properties.description: Returns the collection of groups within the timeline.

related_samples.name: Extrude Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

related_samples.name: Fillet Feature Edit API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates editing a fillet feature. To successfully run this sample you can use this Ruled Surface Feature API Sample

classType.title: Timeline.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteAllAfterMarker.title: Timeline.deleteAllAfterMarker Method
deleteAllAfterMarker.description: Deletes all objects in the timeline that are after the current position of the marker.

item.title: Timeline.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified item in the timeline using an index into the collection. The items are returned in the order they appear in the timeline.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

related_samples.name: Extrude Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

related_samples.name: Ruled Surface Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new ruled surface feature.

moveToBeginning.title: Timeline.moveToBeginning Method
moveToBeginning.description: Moves the marker to the beginning of the timeline.
moveToBeginning.return_description: Returns true if the move is successful

moveToEnd.title: Timeline.moveToEnd Method
moveToEnd.description: Moves the marker to the end of the timeline.
moveToEnd.return_description: Returns true if the move is successful

related_samples.name: Extrude Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

related_samples.name: Fillet Feature Edit API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates editing a fillet feature. To successfully run this sample you can use this Ruled Surface Feature API Sample

moveToPreviousStep.title: Timeline.moveToPreviousStep Method
moveToPreviousStep.description: Moves the marker to the previous step in the timeline.
moveToPreviousStep.return_description: Returns true if the move is successful

movetoNextStep.title: Timeline.movetoNextStep Method
movetoNextStep.description: Moves the marker to the next step in the timeline.
movetoNextStep.return_description: Returns true if the move is successful

play.title: Timeline.play Method
play.description: Plays the timeline beginning at the current position of the marker.
play.return_description: Returns true if playing the timeline was successful

count.title: Timeline.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of items in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = timeline_var.count

isValid.title: Timeline.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = timeline_var.isValid

markerPosition.title: Timeline.markerPosition Property
markerPosition.description: Gets and sets the current position of the marker where 0 is at the beginning of the timeline and the value of Timeline.count is the end of the timeline.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = timeline_var.markerPosition

# Set the value of the property.
timeline_var.markerPosition = propertyValue

objectType.title: Timeline.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = timeline_var.objectType

timelineGroups.title: Timeline.timelineGroups Property
timelineGroups.description: Returns the collection of groups within the timeline.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = timeline_var.timelineGroups
